title: Memorial Day market close: US biotech rally pauses as longevity field digests dual rapamycin signals
description: With NYSE and Nasdaq closed Monday for Memorial Day, longevity-adjacent biotech ETFs hold Friday's mixed close. The pause arrives as the field reconciles two opposing rapamycin signals from the same week — RAPA-EX-01 showing weekly dosing blunts exercise gains, and the U of Arizona / UT San Antonio Phase 3 launches betting on optimized low-dose protocols.

title: Fight Aging's Sunday roundup confirms the field's pivot to precision senolytics, KDM clocks and exercise
description: Sunday's weekly digest in Fight Aging consolidates 12 May 25 entries around three converging themes: senolytics moving from broad killing to precision clearance of pathogenic subpopulations; KDM clocks outperforming epigenetic clocks for short-window dietary intervention response; and physical activity meta-analytically validated as the largest population-scale lever on epigenetic age.

title: Retro Biosciences $1.8B valuation crystallizes capital concentration in autophagy/protein-clearance modality
description: Friday's confirmed $1.8B raise positions Retro alongside Altos Labs and Calico as the third major private longevity capital pool, with August Alzheimer's autophagy-enhancer Phase 1 readout now the next major industry catalyst. The deal pulls non-reprogramming geroscience into the unicorn tier and clarifies that protein-aggregate clearance is the next clinical proving ground.

title: Platelets formally join senescent cells and immune cells as druggable inflammaging node
description: Nature Communications paper showing platelet-specific HuR/ELAVL1 deletion reduces secreted pro-inflammatory factors and delays systemic aging in aged mice promotes platelets to a third major druggable inflammaging axis. The finding opens a new therapeutic target class beyond senolytics and immunosenescence modulators, with read-throughs to cardiovascular and metabolic aging programs.

title: NG101 SCI Phase 2b results provide first MRI proof small molecules can rescue CNS structure
description: Nature Communications publication of NervGen NG101 Phase 2b spinal cord injury data shows faster lesion volume reduction and slower decline in spinal cord cross-sectional area and myelin-sensitive MRI measures vs placebo. First MRI-based evidence that a small molecule can produce structural CNS rescue in chronic trauma, with mechanistic read-throughs to age-related neurodegenerative programs now plausible rather than speculative.

title: Sirtuin 1 Expression and Exercise
description: Cross-tissue, cross-species review connects SIRT1 expression to the improved metabolism following exercise — exercise raises SIRT1 mRNA, activity, and protein in muscle, brain, heart, and adipose.

title: NG101 Enhances Regeneration in Human Spinal Cord Injury
description: Phase 2b participants on NG101 showed faster lesion volume reduction and slower decline of spinal cord cross-sectional area and myelin-sensitive MRI indices vs placebo — first small-molecule structural CNS rescue in chronic human SCI.

title: Short-Term Dietary Intervention Alters Physiological Profiles Relevant to Ageing
description: 104 participants aged 65-75 completed a 4-week diet intervention; KDM deltaAge dropped in omnivorous/high-carb and semi-vegetarian groups vs high-fat controls — KDM clocks outperform epigenetic panels for short-window trials.

title: Aging, M Cells, Gut Microbiome and Intestinal Mucosal Immunity
description: Aged mice showed epithelial barrier dysfunction, altered immune cell populations, inflammatory bacterial enrichment, and impaired M cell function — intestinal aging behaves as an integrated immune/microbiota syndrome.

title: Microglia, TREM2 and Retinal Degeneration in Age-Related Macular Degeneration
description: Review proposes retinal myeloid cells and TREM2 signaling as drivers of dry and neovascular AMD and as therapeutic targets, with implications for age-related vision loss.

title: Cellular Senescence: From Pathogenic Mechanisms to Precision Anti-Aging Interventions
description: Senescent cells are functionally heterogeneous; future senolytic strategies should focus on precision clearance of pathogenic subpopulations rather than blanket marker-based elimination.

title: Platelets as Drivers of Systemic Aging
description: Platelet-specific deletion of HuR/ELAVL1 in aged mice reduced platelet-secreted pro-inflammatory factors, decreased tissue infiltration, and delayed systemic aging — platelets emerge as druggable inflammaging node.

title: LPS Priming Restores Wound Repair in Aged Skin
description: A short pre-injury LPS pulse markedly accelerated wound repair in aged mice via enhanced immune cell activity, keratinocyte responsiveness, and barrier reconstitution.

title: Older Mice May Offer New Insight Into Cancer and Aging
description: In a melanoma model, middle-aged mice showed greatest metastatic spread linked to reduced gamma delta T cell infiltration and tumor-driven immune suppression — relevant for age-appropriate cancer models.

title: Role of Aging on Gamma Delta T Cells in Metastatic Cutaneous Melanoma
description: Middle-aged mice had increased lung and liver metastasis; gamma delta T cell depletion or PROS1 overexpression probed age-related metastatic progression.

title: Physical Activity and DNA Methylation Biological Age: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
description: 44 studies, 145,465 participants: higher physical activity correlated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration; no effect on Hannum or PhenoAge — largest synthesis to date.

title: Social Organization and Mammalian Lifespan
description: Re-analysis of 1,436 mammal species: group-living and pair-living species live longer than solitary species after controlling for body mass and phylogeny.

title: Retro Biosciences raises at $1.8 billion valuation
description: Sam Altman-backed Retro Biosciences confirmed new raise; running first clinical trial — an oral pill enhancing protein-aggregate clearance in Alzheimer's. No dose-limiting toxicities seen; initial data expected around August.

title: Rapamycin and Exercise: New Study Finds Potential Performance Decline
description: RAPA-EX-01 RCT: older adults on weekly rapamycin completed ~2 fewer chair-stand reps, walked ~16 feet less, produced ~2.6 lb less grip force vs placebo; CRP rose; no biological age change.

title: Can a Drug Slow Aging? The Rapamycin Story
source: R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy
description: U of Arizona launches 6-year Phase 3 double-blind low-dose rapamycin trial in older adults, evaluating frailty transitions and IL-6 inflammaging — funded by $12M Coit gift.

title: UT Health San Antonio launches rapamycin clinical trial for healthy aging
description: NIA-funded multi-phase rapamycin/everolimus study in 84 healthy adults 65-90 evaluating dosing, safety, and long-term effects of mTOR inhibition for healthspan.

title: Methylene Blue May Help Protect Hair Follicle Stem Cells from GLP-1 Stress
description: Maryland team shows methylene blue boosts HFSC proliferation via β-catenin activation, synergizes with minoxidil, and protects stem cells from GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced cell death — mechanistic counter for semaglutide/tirzepatide hair thinning.

title: Extending Healthspan Through Public Health and Longevity Medicine
description: Editorial argues healthy longevity requires coordinated integration of public health, primary prevention, geroscience pharmaceuticals, and individualized longevity medicine.

title: Tozorakimab meets primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 monoclonal hit primary endpoint in MIRANDA Phase 3 COPD trial — validating IL-33 blockade as a disease-modifying mechanism for a major disease of aging.

title: BioAge reports positive Phase 1 data for BGE-102
description: BioAge Labs reports positive Phase 1 results for BGE-102 NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor program, supporting further cardiometabolic / inflammaging development.

title: Pulmatrix and Eos SENOLYTIX Announce Merger Agreement
description: Pulmatrix (PULM) acquires Eos SENOLYTIX with $19M concurrent financing to advance MitoXcel mitochondrial peptide platform and lead candidate PTC-2105 for sarcopenia and age-related disease.

title: Insilico forms Longevity Board to oversee AI aging research
description: Insilico stands up dedicated Longevity Board to oversee AI-driven aging research program, formalizing geroscience commitment alongside the $2.75B Eli Lilly deal.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026: Berlin congress reframes aging as loss of system coordination
description: 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity (Berlin, April 8-9) consolidates view that aging is progressive loss of coordination between biological systems — mitochondrial signaling, microbiota-brain axis, metabolic environments, and comparative biology.

title: Recommendations for biomarker data collection in clinical trials by geroscience
description: Guidelines for biomarker data collection in geroscience clinical trials to standardize stratification, intervention prioritization, and response monitoring — useful framework for current rapamycin Phase 3 and Retro autophagy programs.

title: Hevolution Foundation expands geroscience grant portfolio amid 2026 funding push
description: Hevolution continues its $1B+ multi-year geroscience commitment with new academic and translational awards, reinforcing capital availability for aging-focused research outside of the venture pipeline.
